title: Cross-modal Oddball Task.
description: A cross-modal oddball task EEG dataset comprising 146 subjects (98 with Parkinson's disease, 48 controls) collected between 2017-2021. Participants performed a directional response task cued by visual arrows, preceded by synchronized visual and auditory pre-cues that varied between standard and oddball conditions. The dataset includes 64-channel EEG recordings and enables investigation of multimodal sensory processing and cognitive control mechanisms in both healthy and neurodegenerative populations.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded using a 64-channel BrainVision cap during a cross-modal oddball task. Each trial consisted of visual and auditory pre-cues presented simultaneously 500ms before a primary visual GO cue (white arrow) requiring directional response. Pre-cue conditions included both standard, oddball visual, or oddball auditory variants. Data collection occurred from 2017-2021.

funding_references.funder_name: and NRSA F32 AG069445-01 to RC. All research protocols were approved by the University of Iowa
